title: Scrap Wood Wall-Mounted Clamp Rack
summary: A minimal scrap-wood rack that mounts to wall studs and keeps bar or pipe clamps off the floor and within reach.
description:
Bar and pipe clamps are awkward to store loose — they roll off shelves and tangle with anything nearby. This rack is deliberately minimal: one horizontal board, two short vertical supports, and a top strip, sized entirely to whatever scrap you have and however many clamps you own.

The **vertical supports set the rack's standoff from the wall**, and that distance needs to clear your deepest clamp's jaw — measure your biggest clamp before cutting these, not after.

Mounting to **two wall studs, not drywall anchors**, matters more here than it looks like it should: a rack loaded with a half-dozen bar clamps is genuinely heavy, and drywall anchors alone will eventually pull out under that sustained weight.

cutList.note: Long enough to span two wall studs — adjust to your stud spacing.
Depth sized to clear your deepest clamp's jaw.

steps.title: Cut the pieces
steps.body: Cut one horizontal board long enough to span two wall studs, two short vertical side supports sized to the depth of your deepest clamp, and one narrow top strip.

steps.title: Assemble the rack
steps.body: Attach the vertical supports to the horizontal board, then attach the top strip across them.

steps.title: Mount and load
steps.body: Mount the assembled rack to two wall studs for strength, then hang clamps by their jaws over the top strip.
